tldr: Replace AppLovin MAX with Google AdMob as your mobile mediation layer, swapping SDKs, ad units and waterfall configuration in each app. The migration is mostly mobile-engineering work and store-release coordination, not data migration.
why_switch: Consolidate ads inside the Google ecosystem alongside Firebase / Play
Use AdMob's Google demand and bidding partners
Different revenue share or payment terms vs AppLovin
Reduce vendor count by reusing existing Google Cloud / Play account
Compliance or procurement preference for Google as a provider
what_to_export: List of apps, platforms and ad units in MAX
Per-network credentials and ad unit IDs
Historical revenue, impression and eCPM reports
Consent and privacy settings (GDPR, CCPA, ATT)
Code references to the MAX SDK and ad unit IDs
export_method: Inside the AppLovin MAX dashboard, list every app, ad unit and ad format, and capture the current waterfall, including manual CPMs or floors. Export historical performance reports as CSV from MAX so you can baseline post-migration revenue. Document each connected network, its account ID and how it is wired into MAX. Note your current consent setup (IAB TCF, GPP, ATT prompt copy) and your existing app-ads.txt entries. In code, search for AppLovin MAX SDK imports, init calls and ad unit constants in iOS, Android, Unity and React Native sources. Keep this inventory as the authoritative spec until AdMob is fully live.
import_method: Create or reuse a Google AdMob account, register your iOS and Android apps, link them to their respective Google Play / App Store identifiers, and create AdMob ad units matching the MAX inventory (banner / interstitial / rewarded / native / app-open). Add the Google Mobile Ads SDK to each platform following the official AdMob integration guide and remove the AppLovin MAX SDK once parity is reached. Configure mediation in the AdMob console for the demand sources you want to keep, providing their credentials. Update app-ads.txt with Google's required lines. Wire consent (UMP SDK or your existing CMP) so non-personalised ad signals reach AdMob correctly. Test with AdMob's test ad units and test devices, ship behind a remote config flag, then ramp traffic and compare against your MAX baseline.
effort: 3-8 weeks per platform end-to-end, including review and ramp
gotchas: An app store release per platform is required; factor in iOS and Android review timelines.
AppLovin is itself a partner network in AdMob; misconfiguring it can double-count or starve fill.
Consent signalling must use Google's UMP or a compatible CMP, otherwise EEA fill drops.
Ad policy enforcement is stricter on AdMob; review creatives and placements against Google's policies.
App-ads.txt must include Google's lines exactly; missing entries silently cut programmatic demand.
Old MAX code left in the binary can cause duplicate impression accounting and crashes.

email.subject: We're moving our app ads to Google AdMob
email.body:
Hi,

In the next 14 days we are switching our mobile ad provider from AppLovin MAX to Google AdMob. The change happens through normal app updates from the App Store and Google Play.

You might notice small differences in the ad experience while the new system optimises. Please install the latest version of the app when it becomes available.

If this raises any account or compliance questions, just reply to this email.

Thanks,
Your customer
